controls.summary: Prompt Injection — controls preventing injected instructions from overriding the system prompt.
evidence: Zero open findings in family prompt-injection / llm-app-security.

controls.summary: Insecure Output Handling — LLM output is not directly piped to dangerous sinks (eval/shell/SQL/HTML).
evidence: Zero open findings in subfamily llm-output-untrusted-sink.

controls.summary: Training Data Poisoning — provenance and integrity controls on training/fine-tuning data.
Model Denial of Service — rate-limit / token-cap controls.
evidence: LLM API calls in source carry an explicit max_tokens cap (bodyguard rule no-max-tokens).

controls.summary: Supply Chain — model & dataset supply-chain integrity.
evidence: Sigstore provenance verification configured if opt-in.

controls.summary: Sensitive Information Disclosure — output filters prevent leakage.
Insecure Plugin / Tool Design — tools the LLM can invoke are narrowly scoped.
evidence: Agent tool definitions reviewed for excessive agency.

controls.summary: Excessive Agency — agent autonomy is bounded by explicit guards.
evidence: MCP server config limits write-tools to confirm:true + reserved-write-path refusal.
No private-key-in-frontend or rpc-key-inline findings in client code.
